data.title: Top 10 stocks according to Seeking Alpha’s quant metrics
data.description:
Wall Street has experienced an up and down trading year in 2025 and finding the correct investment can be a challenge. Therefore, Seeking Alpha spotlighted its top 10 ranked stocks according to its quant metrics.

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Outlined below are the top 10 ranked stocks according to Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings. 

No. 10: Allianz SE (OTCPK:ALIZY), quant grade of 4.98.

No. 9: Allot Ltd. (NASDAQ:ALLT), quant grade of 4.98.

No. 8: QuantaSing Group Limited (NASDAQ:QSG), quant grade of 4.98.

No. 7: Aeva Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEVA), quant grade of 4.98.

No. 6: OppFi Inc. (NYSE:OPFI), quant grade of 4.98.

No. 5: Heritage Insurance Holdings (NYSE:HRTG), quant grade of 4.99.

No. 4: IHS Holding Limited (NYSE:IHS), quant grade of 4.99.

No. 3: Nutex Health (NASDAQ:NUTX), quant grade of 4.99. 

No. 2: Microvast Holdings (NASDAQ:MVST), quant grade of 4.99.

No. 1: LexinFintech Holdings (NASDAQ:LX), quant grade of 4.99. 

Year-to-date price action: ALIZY +26.9%, ALLT +36.6%, QSG +237.5%, AEVA +248.8%, OPFI +48.4%, HRTG +98.8%, HIS +108.9%, NUTX +446.6%, MVST +59.6%, and LX +51.3%,

data.title: Wall Street is mixed as Cisco boosts Dow, offsetting Walmart weakness
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday is mixed, helped by gains in defensive sectors such as consumer staples and utilities. Sentiment was under pressure due to a combination of buyer exhaustion and some lackluster economic data. U.S. Treasury yields fell as traders snapped up bonds.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last at +0.34% at 5,911.87 points, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.31% at 19,091.22 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.59% at 42,299.14 points.

U.S. stocks are attempting to extend a three-day rally sparked by a surprise trade deal between the U.S. and China and a benign consumer inflation report. 

"The strong recent equity rally showed some signs of exhaustion over the past 24 hours, even though gains for the tech mega caps helped the S&P 500 (+0.10%) extend its gains since the closing low on April 7 to +18.26%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said. 

Earlier, before the opening bell on Thursday, investors received a slew of economic indicators. First up was the April producer price index (PPI) report, with both headline and core producer prices unexpectedly declining on a M/M basis. 

At the same time, the U.S. Census Bureau said retail sales in April edged up +0.1% M/M, just above the consensus for a flat reading. Core retail sales also increased by a similar amount, but came in below the expected rise.   

"Because it largely represents goods spending, which comprises less than a third of overall personal consumption in the United States, the retail sales report is not typically an A-lister among economic indicators…until now," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said. 

"For the next few months at least, the retail sales report is set to be center stage as we seek to measure the impact of tariffs. While import duties can introduce pass-through effect to the service sector, it is goods that are apt to be the most impacted. We may eventually see these impacts manifested through product scarcity or higher prices, but the first action will simply be evident in the sales activity," Quinlan added. 

Additionally, two measures of manufacturing activity continued to show weakness in the sector. 

Also weighing on sentiment on Thursday was Walmart (WMT), -1.1%. The U.S. retailing giant's management on an earnings conference call warned that tariffs could lead to higher prices for consumers. 

Offsetting some of Walmart's (WMT) weakness was Cisco (CSCO). Shares of the networking giant were +4.6% after its solid quarterly results and guidance.  

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 8 basis points to 4.45%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 8 basis points to 3.97%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: Lantern Pharma outlines commercialization of radar AI modules and expects LP-184 trial enrollment completion by June 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lantern Pharma Inc. (LTRN) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Panna Sharma stated that "the first quarter of 2025 represents a pivotal inflection point for Lantern Pharma." He highlighted significant progress in the clinical portfolio and the expansion of the proprietary radar AI platform to over 200 billion oncology-focused data points. He emphasized, "Our clinical pipeline progress, our AI platform advancements, and our initiatives to maximize shareholder value" as three primary pillars for the quarter.
  • Sharma reported ongoing advancement of multiple clinical programs, including the Phase 1a trial for LP-184, which is expected to complete enrollment with 62 to 65 patients by June 2025. He noted the FDA clearance of two clinical trial protocols for LP-184, including a "Phase 1b/2 study in TNBC evaluating LP-184, both as monotherapy and in combination with the PARP inhibitor, Olaparib," and another in a biomarker-defined subset of drug-resistant non-small cell lung cancer.
  • The company is preparing to open up its radar AI platform, starting with a "freemium type approach," aiming to drive collaborations and economics for Lantern.
  • Sharma described the company's cash position as "approximately $19.7 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities," and indicated a runway through at least mid-May 2026.
  • CFO David Margrave stated, "Our general and administrative expenses were approximately $1.51 million for the first quarter of 2025... R&D expenses were approximately $3.3 million... The decrease was primarily due to reductions in CRO and clinical site costs for LP-184... We recorded a net loss of approximately $4.5 million for the first quarter of 2025 or $0.42 per share."

Outlook

  • Sharma indicated the company expects to "complete enrollment with 62 to 65 patients across a wide range of solid tumors by the June 2025" for the LP-184 Phase 1a trial, with comprehensive data readouts to follow.
  • The company plans to commercialize initial modules from the radar platform, beginning with the blood brain barrier (BBB) permeability prediction tool.
  • Sharma stated, "We expect to initially do this as a freemium type approach... a road map that we'll be discussing, probably toward the end of this quarter or early next quarter."
  • The company maintains a focus on fiscal discipline and expects its current cash resources to provide an operating runway through at least mid-May 2026.

Financial Results

  • General and administrative expenses were approximately $1.51 million for Q1 2025.
  • R&D expenses were approximately $3.3 million for Q1 2025, down from $4.3 million in Q1 2024.
  • The company reported a net loss of approximately $4.5 million for Q1 2025 or $0.42 per share, compared to a net loss of $5.4 million or $0.51 per share for Q1 2024.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled approximately $19.7 million as of March 31, 2025.
  • Lantern Pharma had 10,784,725 shares of common stock outstanding and a fully diluted share count of approximately 12.1 million as of March 31, 2025.

Q&A

  • An unidentified participant asked about the commercialization plans for the radar AI modules. Sharma responded that the company will "start with a freemium type approach" and that the strategy is to drive broader collaborations and aggregate additional data to strengthen the platform, adding, "We have a road map that we'll be discussing, probably toward the end of this quarter or early next quarter."
  • The same participant sought clarification on the HARMONIC trial, specifically regarding enrollment. Sharma clarified, "The expansion cohort will be both U.S. and Asian, but the expansion cohort is randomized... it's not the Asian cohort. It's just the Asian patients as part of the expansion cohort."
  • On LP-184 trial progress, Sharma stated, "We expect the trial to be fully enrolled next month... we'll have preliminary data after we start getting the clinical data, the biomarker correlations, etc., shortly after that."
  • On the potential for FDA use of AI, Sharma commented, "I want to believe that the FDA, will definitely have to use AI in its evaluation of scientific literature, data... I think it'll help to bring down some of the costs of the FDA and hopefully speed things up."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in the commercialization of radar AI and clinical trial progress, with a neutral tone, focusing on clarification and business model implications.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone, emphasizing operational efficiency, disciplined capital management, and the transformative potential of its AI platform. Sharma used phrases such as "We believe..." and "We expect..." reflecting cautious optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remains consistently inquisitive and management continues to project confidence and highlight milestones, though this quarter places increased emphasis on near-term commercialization of AI modules.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company advanced from over 100 billion to approximately 200 billion oncology data points in its radar AI platform, marking a substantial increase in platform capabilities.
  • Strategic focus shifted more prominently toward commercialization of AI modules, with explicit plans for a freemium model and a broader rollout discussed in greater detail than the previous quarter.
  • Clinical milestones remain centered on LP-184 and LP-300, with timelines for enrollment and data readouts clarified further this quarter.
  • Financial discipline remains a key theme, with a decrease in R&D expenses and net loss compared to the same period last year.
  • Analyst questions this quarter centered on AI commercialization and trial enrollment specifics, while the previous quarter included more detailed questions about ADC programs and dose levels.
  • Management's confidence in AI-driven cost and timeline reductions persists, with added focus on monetization and partnership opportunities compared to the last quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management reiterated the need for additional funding in the near future, with Margrave stating, "We will need additional funding in the near future, and one of our key objectives is to pursue additional funding opportunities."
  • Sharma acknowledged potential risks in deploying AI modules and the regulatory environment, particularly regarding FDA adoption of AI methodologies, emphasizing a cautious approach as new technologies are integrated.
  • Analyst concerns focused on the scalability and monetization of the radar platform, as well as clarity around clinical trial design and enrollment.

Final Takeaway

Lantern Pharma's Q1 2025 update underscores a pivotal period marked by advancements in clinical trial enrollment, aggressive expansion and planned commercialization of its radar AI platform, and continued operational discipline. The company is focused on completing key clinical milestones, including LP-184 trial enrollment by June 2025, while simultaneously opening new revenue streams through AI technology. Management reiterates confidence in its dual-engine approach to precision oncology and AI, while also noting the necessity of future funding and the importance of strategic partnerships to accelerate growth and maximize shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Engaged Capital's Q1 moves include new stake in Yeti, GXO, exits PRA Group
data.description:
Engaged Capital exited its stakes in PRA Group (NASDAQ:PRAA) and took new positions in Yeti Holdings (NYSE:YETI) and GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) in Q1 2025, among other moves.

The hedge fund, known for its investor activism, disclosed the information in its latest 13F filing for the three months ended March 31, 2025, published on Thursday.

The fund picked up 1.54M shares in the distributor of outdoor products Yeti Holdings (YETI) and 912,000 shares in the supply chain management firm GXO Logistics (GXO).

The fund reduced its holdings in NCR Voyix (NYSE:VYX) and Portillo's (NASDAQ:PTLO). The activist investor recently reached an agreement with Portillo's (PTLO).

In other moves, it raised its stake in cryogenic gas liquefier Chart by 68% to 368,996 shares and Vans sneakers maker VF Corp (NYSE:VFC) to 5.48M from 3.48M.

The activist fund maintained its position in BRC (NYSE:BRCC).

data.title: Global Water Resources targets $6.5M rate increase as industrial and multifamily growth continues
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Global Water Resources, Inc. (GWRS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • President and CEO Ron Fleming reported a 4.3% increase in total active service connections to 65,163 as of March 31, 2025, maintaining an annualized 4% growth rate. He stated, "Year-to-date, we have invested $15.2 million into infrastructure improvements and existing utilities to provide safe and reliable service." Fleming highlighted that most planned investments in 2025 relate to post-test year projects in Santa Cruz Water Company and Palo Verde Utilities Company, intended for inclusion in the 2024 test year rate application.

  • Fleming discussed a decline in single-family building permits in the Phoenix metro area and Maricopa for Q1 2025 versus the prior year but noted, "It is important to note that a single quarter, specifically Q1, is not a trend as timing of permits being pulled can be influenced by many factors." He emphasized a record number of multifamily projects in development and continued strong industrial investments, referencing the "most recent $100 billion announcement on the additional planned expansions at TSMC," which is in addition to previous commitments.

  • Fleming outlined the signing of a special industrial contract with Procter & Gamble in December 2024 and ongoing discussions with other large firms. He described the Maricopa industrial complex as another future growth opportunity and added, "Layering new rates on top of all of this growth could drive meaningful revenue and earnings growth in the years to come."

  • CFO Mike Liebman reported, "Total revenue for the first quarter of 2025 was $12.5 million, which was up $0.8 million or 7.3% compared to Q1 2024." He attributed revenue growth to "a 4.3% increase in active service connections and increased consumption." Operating expenses were $11.2 million, up $0.9 million from Q1 2024, due to higher depreciation, increased power, chemicals, and repairs, as well as contract IT and labor services. Liebman continued, "Net income for Q1 2025 was $0.6 million or $0.02 per diluted share as compared to $0.7 million or $0.03 per diluted share in Q1 2024."

  • Liebman also highlighted a $32 million equity capital raise in March and an extension of the line of credit to May 2027, increasing it to $20 million. He noted, "When combining the $31.5 million of cash and the $20 million unused revolver, we have over $50 million of liquidity to support our capital expenditure program as well as the Tucson acquisition."

  • COO Chris Krygier confirmed, "The Arizona Corporation Commission approved new rates for our farmers water utility. We started charging those new rates on May 1, so you will see those results reflected in future quarters." Krygier added that the company filed for a $6.5 million net revenue increase for Santa Cruz and Palo Verde on March 5, 2025, and expects hearings to begin in December.

Outlook

  • Management expects the continued layering of new rates and large-scale growth projects to drive future revenue and earnings. Fleming stated, "We believe 2025 will be another solid year for Global Water, putting the four ways we grow our utility company together... you can see how Global Water is going to be able to grow considerably in the years to come."

  • Krygier reported, "We filed our Santa Cruz and Palo Verde rate case on March 5, 2025, requesting a net revenue increase of $6.5 million. The case is off to a solid start."

  • The company targets closing the Tucson acquisition in the summer of 2025.

Financial Results

  • Liebman disclosed, "Adjusted EBITDA was $5.6 million in Q1 2025 compared to $5.4 million in Q1 2024. This is an increase of approximately $0.2 million or 4.4%."

  • Operating expenses rose to $11.2 million, with notable increases in depreciation and O&M costs due to capital investments and higher consumption.

  • The company reported a successful equity raise and increased liquidity to support capital expenditures and acquisitions.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The transcript does not contain any analyst questions or management responses in a Q&A session for Q1 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' sentiment cannot be assessed for Q1 2025 as no analyst questions were present in the transcript.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout the prepared remarks, emphasizing continued growth and resilience. Fleming stated, "Customer growth, revenue growth, rate base growth and earnings growth have all been exceptionally strong, and we expect that to continue."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remains positive, with management highlighting both growth achievements and future opportunities, though they acknowledge ongoing economic headwinds and stock price pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Active service connection growth remained steady at 4% annualized, with a slight decrease from the previous quarter's year-end figure of 4.4%.

  • The company shifted its strategic focus to executing on newly approved rate increases and advancing a $6.5 million proposed rate increase for 2025.

  • Management continued to cite strong multifamily and industrial development as key growth drivers, consistent with prior quarters, but noted a short-term pullback in single-family permits.

  • Liquidity improved with a $32 million equity raise and increased line of credit, compared to no such mention in the previous quarter.

  • The previous quarter included more detail on the pending Tucson acquisition and a focus on regulatory innovation (Cost of Service Adjustment), which was less emphasized this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Fleming acknowledged a "pullback" in single-family building permits and noted macroeconomic uncertainty, including tariffs and inflation, as factors impacting short-term growth.

  • Management addressed rising operating expenses, highlighting the impact of inflation, increased capital investments, and higher consumption-driven costs.

  • Fleming stated, "Yes, client inflation and other cost drivers have caught up with us and are impacting our earnings growth."

  • Regulatory and rate case outcomes remain a focus, with management monitoring timing and process milestones for approval and implementation.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that despite a short-term decline in single-family permits and rising costs, Global Water Resources remains optimistic about long-term growth, supported by multifamily and industrial demand, new rate implementations, and a strengthened liquidity position. The company expects recent rate activity and continued infrastructure investment to drive meaningful revenue and earnings gains in the coming years, while actively managing macroeconomic and regulatory challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Jefferies feels positive on Twilio's conversational AI, multi-year Microsoft agreement
data.description:
Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) held its annual flagship customer conference, SIGNAL, on Wednesday in San Francisco, and the analysts at Jefferies left the event feeling "incrementally positive" on the new partnerships and offerings by the customer engagement platform provider.

Twilio emphasized the opportunity it has in the conversational artificial intelligence space. The company highlighted recent strategic partnerships and customer wins, including those with OpenAI, Sierra and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT).

"Mgmt highlighted that TWLO's value add is being able to solve for the inherent complexity in voice networks for these vendors, a space TWLO has been investing in for yrs," said Jefferies analysts Samad Samana and Billy Fitzsimmons, in an investor note. "Second, mgmt emphasized that it is able to deliver a cheaper (and often faster) conversational AI agent than some competitors. TWLO's AI agents will reference just a subset of the end customer's data, rather than needing to query the open internet."

Twilio's main conversational AI product offerings include ConversationRelay and Conversational Intelligence. The first allows clients to deploy voice-based AI agents with whatever large language model they prefer. The second product analyzes text and voice conversations to deliver insights to businesses.

The company also plans to introduce a new central console later this year.

"The console will have multiple channels in a single interface and is being designed to reduce customers' friction to adopting incremental channels/products," Saman noted. "Mgmt highlighted that the console will have active prompts and a teaching component to help ensure customers better understand the breadth of TWLO's portfolio. Second, alongside this higher level of integration, TWLO plans on introducing bundles. The firm is piloting 4-5 various product bundles today, which will be introduced to the broader customer base over the next 18 months."

Jefferies has a Hold rating on Twilio and a $122 price target.

data.title: Trade policy uncertainty casts shadow over otherwise 'solid' economy, Fed's Barr says
data.description:
While Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr is maintaining that the U.S. economy stands on "solid footing," high uncertainty from President Donald Trump's trade policies is clouding the outlook and dampening consumer and business confidence, he cautioned on Thursday.

"In my view, the economy is on solid footing, with solid growth, low and stable unemployment, and inflation continuing to come down towards our 2 percent target," the former Fed vice chair of supervision said in prepared opening remarks via pre-recorded video for an event hosted by the New York Fed.

"But the outlook has been clouded by trade policies that have led to an increase in uncertainty, contributing to declines in measures of consumer and business sentiment," he added.

Barr noted that potential disruptions to supply chains and distribution networks pose an outsized disadvantage to small businesses, which tend to be less diversified with limited access to credit, and are thus more exposed to economic shocks.

"As we saw during the pandemic, such disruptions can have large and lasting effects on prices, as well as output. If these disruptions were to occur, we'd likely see lower growth and higher inflation ahead," he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: UnitedHealth defended as stock hits five-year low after report on DoJ probe
data.description:
Seeking Alpha analysts piled up bullish views on UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) as the stock crashed ~13% on Thursday after a report on a criminal investigation initiated by the Department of Justice added to the company’s latest woes.

While the Eden Prairie, Minnesota-based managed care giant denied any knowledge of such a probe as reported by The Wall Street Journal on Wednesday, its shares extended a multiday decline amid mounting investor concerns, especially after the company’s abrupt leadership change early this week.

While UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) is not new to intense regulatory scrutiny given its role as the largest health insurer in the U.S., the company has faced multiple issues in recent months, the most notable of which is the tragic murder of Brian Thompson, CEO of its insurance unit, in December.

The company's setbacks continued with a rare earnings miss and a guidance cut in April amid a spike in medical costs, and the recent CEO change, which coincided with a decision to pull the full-year outlook on Tuesday, has already prompted Bank of America to downgrade UNH to Neutral from Buy.

However, Seeking Alpha analyst StockBros Research is not yet ready for a bearish thesis on the stock despite its ~48% YTD decline. While the DoJ investigation adds to the risks and could undermine UNH’s potential, StockBros Research wrote that "I'm willing to take this risk at a 'derisked' valuation.

“There have been many cases that have resulted in settlements instead of criminal charges. This could be one of them,” the analyst added, citing the stock’s attractive valuation and reiterating the Buy rating on the stock.

SA Investing Group Leader JR Research agreed. “Despite the turmoil, UNH now trades at a deep discount to healthcare peers, with risks largely priced after falling more than 50% from 2024 highs,” the analyst wrote, reiterating the Buy recommendation.

Meanwhile, Investing Group Leader Danil Sereda argued that the UNH’s selloff is overdone, and citing past DoJ investigations into business practices of companies such as Nvidia (NVDA), the analyst said that the stock can rebound sharply once the dust settles.

“Even if the probe comes, some historical market reactions to similar probes (e.g., SMCI, NVDA) suggest sharp recoveries once panic selling subsides,” Sareda added with a Buy rating on UNH.

Setting a 12-month price target of $400-$450, SA Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov also issued a Buy on UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) but warned that investors should expect continuing volatility and downside risk.

“Despite massive recent weakness and uncertainty, I believe UNH is a 'reluctant' buy due to its cash flow, dividend, and systemic importance,” Dergunov added.

However, according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant System, which beats the market consistently, UNH is a Hold despite a relatively compelling valuation, due mainly to unfavorable earnings revisions and its below-par earnings and cashflow growth.

data.title: Boot Barn enjoys a degree of stability not enjoyed by retailers - analysts
data.description:
Boot Barn’s (NYSE:BOOT) strong comparable sales growth and outlook for the current quarter led to a 20% rally in the stock on Thursday and a four-month high that makes it the best performing stock in the apparel/retail category (excluding Foot Locker’s merger-fueled 85% gain) on Thursday.

The performance may seem overdone, considering BOOT’s results were not overwhelmingly bullish. The company missed top- and bottom-line expectations and issued a below-consensus outlook for the current fiscal year.

But the solid comparable same store sales came despite macro headwinds and a weak February and were driven almost entirely by transactions.

“Momentum continues at BOOT,” said BTIG analyst Janine Stichter. “The strength was broad-based, with all channels, geos, and major merchandise categories trending positively.”

BOOT remains one of the last remaining growth stories in retail. Its heavily need-based assortment and lack of fashion or seasonal risk offers a degree of relative stability not enjoyed by most retailers.

This allows for a consistently steady performance even during periods of volatility, Stichter writes.

Jefferies’ Carey Tarlowe agrees. “We remain bullish on BOOT’s ability to gain or maintain market share while managing healthy margins despite the demand uncertainty ahead.”

During the company’s earnings call, CEO John Hazen laid out his expansion plans despite the ongoing tariff uncertainty and a cloudy consumer outlook with a goal of 65 to 70 new stores.

“Given the consistent performance of our new store openings across all geographies, we believe that we have the market potential to double our store count in the U.S. alone over the next several years.”

No doubt the company is exploiting the “country lifestyle” fashion trend and should continue to expect “tailwinds from strong Country and Holiday markets and witness fewer industry-wide macro headwinds versus other specialty retailers,” Tarlowe adds.

This could drive store count to triple in the foreseeable future, BTIG’s Stichter says, aided by growth in its private brand and loyal customer base.

Stichter and Tarlowe are both bullish on Boot Barn (NYSE:BOOT) with a Buy rating and price targets that assume a 50% and 41% upside to Wednesday’s closing price. This outlook is shared by Wall Street analysts who rate the stock as a Strong Buy, while Seeking Alpha authors view Boot Barn (NYSE:BOOT) as a Buy.

Boot Barn (BOOT) shares have risen by nearly 39% year over year compared a more modest gain of 12% in the S&P 500.

data.title: Gee Group signals return to profitability in late 2025 as M&A accelerates and cost cuts deepen
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: GEE Group Inc. (JOB) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Derek Dewan, Chairman and CEO, outlined that "we have encountered and continue to face very difficult and challenging conditions in the hiring environment for our staffing services" due to over hiring in 2021-2022 and ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty. Dewan emphasized, "We finalized our plans to sell the company's former Industrial Staffing Services segment in the quarter and are actively negotiating the sale currently."
  • Dewan stated, "We have ramped up our M&A activities and completed our first such transaction in the quarter and are in the process of evaluation and diligence on several others." He also highlighted initiatives to "streamline our core operations, significantly reducing costs and improving the productivity of our field personnel."
  • Dewan projected, "Our goal and expectation is to become profitable again in the latter part of 2025 or early in 2026."
  • Dewan reaffirmed, "We also continue to believe that our stock is undervalued and especially so based upon recent trading at levels very near and even slightly below tangible book value."
  • Kim Thorpe, Senior Vice President and CFO, reported, "Consolidated revenues for the quarter and year-to-date were $24.5 million and $48.5 million, down 4% and 10%, respectively from the comparable prior periods." Thorpe added, "SG&A for the quarter were $9.3 million, down 3% as compared with the prior year quarter." Thorpe explained, "Our current or working capital ratio as of March 31, 2025, was a robust 3.9:1. Free cash flow...for the fiscal year and first half was a negative $1.1 million as compared with positive cash flow of $400,000 for the fiscal 2024 first half."

Outlook

  • Management aims for a return to profitability in the latter part of 2025 or early to mid-2026, with Dewan stating, "Our goal and expectation is to become profitable again in the latter part of 2025 or early in 2026."
  • Thorpe reiterated, "Our goal is to return to profitability, as Derek mentioned, in the latter part of 2025 and early to mid-2026."
  • Plans include "significantly lowering our SG&A expenses going forward accordingly" and continued M&A activity, as well as the integration of AI and offshore recruiting to improve efficiency.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated revenues were $24.5 million for the quarter, with gross profits and gross margins of $8.4 million and 34.1%, respectively.
  • Net loss from continuing operations was $33 million or $0.30 per diluted share, driven primarily by a $22 million noncash goodwill impairment charge and a $9.9 million noncash valuation allowance related to deferred tax assets.
  • Professional contract staffing services revenues were $21.5 million for the quarter, direct hire placement revenues were $3 million, with the latter up 22% for the quarter.
  • SG&A for the quarter was $9.3 million, and the company reported negative $600,000 in adjusted EBITDA.
  • The company reported $18.7 million in cash and an undrawn ABL facility of $7.4 million, with no outstanding debt, and a net book value per share of $0.46 as of March 31, 2025.

Q&A

  • An analyst inquired about the M&A pipeline and number of deals under review; management responded that "the pipeline is robust and full" and confirmed there are outstanding letters of intent and that they "expect to get M&A done in this fiscal year."
  • Questions on share repurchases and stock buybacks were addressed by Dewan: "We had a deep discussion of both M&A and share repurchases...both are on the list of enhancing shareholder value."
  • On the sale of the industrial business, Thorpe explained, "Actually, the sale was commissioned as part of the strategic alternatives review last April. It took some time...but more or less, we think that it's gone very well and that the end result will be good and it should close sooner."
  • Questions on SG&A reduction led Thorpe to detail, "Two things in particular that we think are going to be very helpful...are more of an assertive move into use of offshore recruiters...and...artificial intelligence, AI."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, pressing on M&A progress, SG&A reductions, and capital allocation, but did not display marked negativity or skepticism.
  • Management's tone was proactive, focusing on aggressive cost controls, strategic M&A, and technology integration, with Dewan stating, "We are working aggressively taking actions to adjust and enhance our strategic focus, growth plans and financial performance and results."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment shifted from cautious optimism to stronger assertiveness regarding strategic actions and a clear timeline for a return to profitability, while analyst sentiment remained steady with a focus on execution and capital deployment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company’s revenue declined from $26 million in Q1 to $24.5 million in Q2, and gross margin improved from 31.9% to 34.1%.
  • SG&A expenses increased as a percentage of revenues, while total SG&A dollars declined.
  • The net loss widened significantly due to noncash impairment and tax valuation charges, a new development not present in the previous quarter.
  • The Q2 call highlighted the finalized plan to sell the industrial segment and increased focus on technology and offshore recruiting for cost reduction, while M&A activity continued from the Hornet Staffing acquisition.
  • Management’s tone became more assertive and explicit about profitability targets, while analysts continued to focus on M&A, cost control, and capital allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted "very difficult and challenging conditions in the hiring environment," citing macroeconomic uncertainty, interest rate volatility, inflation, and accelerated AI adoption.
  • Dewan noted, "These conditions have continued to have a cooling effect upon job orders for both temporary help and direct hire placements."
  • The significant net loss was driven by a $22 million noncash goodwill impairment charge and a $9.9 million valuation allowance for deferred tax assets.
  • Cost reduction remains a priority, with a focus on offshore recruiting and AI to mitigate SG&A pressures.

Final Takeaway

GEE Group’s management emphasized renewed aggressiveness in cost control, M&A, and operational streamlining amid challenging industry conditions, with a stated goal to restore profitability by late 2025 or early 2026. The company is progressing with the sale of its industrial division, implementing technology-driven efficiencies, and maintaining a strong liquidity position to support both organic and acquisitive growth strategies, as it works to position itself for an anticipated recovery in the staffing market and improved shareholder value.
